description: privault — Local-only AES-256-GCM encrypted personal vault for passwords, health records, banking info, and secure notes. Zero cloud, zero telemetry, AI-resistant.

userStories.title: Scaffold project structure with pyproject.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project skeleton so that all other stories have a valid package to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml exists with name='privault', requires-python='>=3.10'
dependencies: typer, cryptography, argon2-cffi, pyperclip, pydantic
dev dependencies: pytest, pytest-cov, mypy, black, isort, flake8
src/privault/__init__.py exists with version='0.1.0'
src/privault/cli.py, crypto.py, vault.py, storage.py, models.py, clipboard.py, audit.py all exist as empty modules
tests/__init__.py and tests/conftest.py exist
[project.scripts] entry: privault = 'privault.cli:app'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement crypto module (Argon2id + AES-256-GCM)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need encryption primitives so that all vault data can be encrypted and decrypted securely.
acceptanceCriteria: crypto.py exports: derive_key(password: str, salt: bytes) -> bytes
derive_key uses Argon2id with memory_cost=65536, time_cost=3, parallelism=4
crypto.py exports: encrypt(key: bytes, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes
crypto.py exports: decrypt(key: bytes, ciphertext: bytes) -> bytes
encrypt uses AES-256-GCM with random 12-byte nonce prepended to output
decrypt raises ValueError on authentication failure (wrong key or tampered data)
generate_salt() -> bytes returns 32 cryptographically random bytes
tests/test_crypto.py: encrypt then decrypt round-trip returns original plaintext
tests/test_crypto.py: decrypt with wrong key raises ValueError
tests/test_crypto.py: two encryptions of same plaintext produce different ciphertext (nonce randomness)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement storage module (encrypted SQLite)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an encrypted storage layer so that all entries are stored as ciphertext in SQLite.
acceptanceCriteria: storage.py exports VaultStorage class with __init__(vault_path: Path, session_key: bytes)
VaultStorage.init_db() creates SQLite tables: vault_meta (salt, version, created_at), entries (id TEXT PK, category TEXT, encrypted_data BLOB, created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT)
VaultStorage.write_entry(entry_id: str, category: str, data: dict) encrypts data JSON and stores blob
VaultStorage.read_entry(entry_id: str) -> dict decrypts and returns data
VaultStorage.list_entries(category: Optional[str]) -> list[dict] returns id+category+created_at only (no decryption)
VaultStorage.delete_entry(entry_id: str) removes from database
tests/test_storage.py: write then read returns original dict
tests/test_storage.py: raw sqlite3 read of the blob is NOT the original plaintext (ciphertext on disk)
tests/test_storage.py: read with wrong key raises ValueError
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement models for all four entry types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed data models so that all entry types are validated before encryption.
acceptanceCriteria: models.py exports PasswordEntry(site: str, username: str, password: str, url: str = '', notes: str = '')
models.py exports HealthEntry(provider: str, entry_type: str, value: str, notes: str = '')
models.py exports BankEntry(institution: str, account_type: str, account_number: str, routing_number: str = '', notes: str = '')
models.py exports NoteEntry(title: str, body: str, tags: list[str] = [])
All models are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses
Each model has a .to_dict() -> dict method
Each model has a classmethod .from_dict(d: dict) -> Self
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement vault session management (init, unlock, lock)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to initialize a new vault and unlock/lock it with my master password.
acceptanceCriteria: vault.py exports VaultSession class
VaultSession.init(vault_path: Path, master_password: str) creates new vault: generates salt, derives key, creates DB, returns session
VaultSession.unlock(vault_path: Path, master_password: str) -> VaultSession reads salt from DB, derives key, validates by reading a known test entry
VaultSession.lock() zeros the session key bytes in memory
VaultSession raises VaultNotFoundError if vault_path does not exist on unlock
VaultSession raises WrongPasswordError if decryption fails on unlock
vault.py exports get_default_vault_path() -> Path returning Path.home() / '.privault' / 'vault.db'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement clipboard module with 30-second auto-clear
userStories.description: As a user, I need secure clipboard copy that automatically clears after 30 seconds so AI/other processes cannot read my secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: clipboard.py exports copy_with_autoclean(secret: str, timeout: int = 30)
Function copies secret to clipboard using pyperclip
Starts a background daemon thread that clears clipboard after timeout seconds
Prints to stderr: 'Copied to clipboard. Will clear in 30 seconds.'
Thread is daemon=True so it does not block process exit
tests/test_clipboard.py: after timeout, clipboard content is empty string
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement audit log module
userStories.description: As a user, I need a tamper-evident local log of every vault access so I can see if anything suspicious happened.
acceptanceCriteria: audit.py exports AuditLog class with __init__(log_path: Path, hmac_key: bytes)
AuditLog.log(action: str, entry_id: str = '', category: str = '') appends: timestamp|action|entry_id|category|HMAC
HMAC is computed over the line content using SHA-256
AuditLog.read() -> list[dict] parses and verifies each line's HMAC; raises AuditTampered on mismatch
tests/test_audit.py: log then read returns same entries
tests/test_audit.py: manually editing a log line causes AuditTampered on read
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement 'privault init' CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I need to create a new vault with a master password from the command line.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.py has @app.command() 'init' that calls VaultSession.init()
Prompts for master password with confirmation (typer.prompt with hide_input=True, confirmation_prompt=True)
Creates ~/.privault/ directory if it does not exist
Sets vault file permissions to 0o600
Prints: 'Vault created at ~/.privault/vault.db'
Raises error and exits if vault already exists (don't overwrite)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault add password' CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I need to interactively add a password entry to my vault.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.py 'add' command has subcommand 'password'
Prompts: site (required), username (required), password (hidden, required), url (optional), notes (optional)
Unlocks vault by prompting for master password
Generates entry ID as uuid4
Encrypts and stores via VaultStorage.write_entry()
Logs action to AuditLog
Prints: 'Password for <site> saved.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault get' CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I need to retrieve an entry and copy its secret to clipboard.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.py 'get' command takes NAME argument (searches site/title/provider/institution)
Unlocks vault by prompting for master password
If multiple matches found, prints numbered list and prompts to choose
For password entries: copies password field to clipboard with 30s auto-clear
For health/bank entries: prints all fields to terminal (no clipboard for structured data)
For note entries: prints body to terminal
Logs access to AuditLog
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault add health', 'privault add bank', 'privault add note' commands
userStories.description: As a user, I need to add health, banking, and note entries to my vault.
acceptanceCriteria: 'add health' prompts: provider (required), entry_type (required), value (hidden, required), notes (optional)
'add bank' prompts: institution (required), account_type (required), account_number (hidden, required), routing_number (optional), notes (optional)
'add note' prompts: title (required), body (opens $EDITOR or prompts multiline), tags (comma-separated, optional)
All three use VaultStorage.write_entry() with correct category
All three log to AuditLog
All three print confirmation on success
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault list' and 'privault search' commands
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see what's in my vault and find specific entries.
acceptanceCriteria: 'list' command unlocks vault and calls VaultStorage.list_entries()
'list --category password|health|bank|note' filters by category
Displays: ID (first 8 chars), category, name/site, created_at — NO secrets printed
'search QUERY' unlocks vault, decrypts all entries, searches site/title/provider/institution/tags
Search is case-insensitive
Search displays same columns as list (no secrets)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault generate' and 'privault lock' commands
userStories.description: As a user, I need to generate strong passwords and manually lock my vault.
acceptanceCriteria: 'generate' command generates a cryptographically random password using secrets module
'generate --length N' defaults to 24 characters
'generate --no-symbols' generates alphanumeric only
Copies generated password to clipboard with 30s auto-clear
Also prints password to terminal so user can see it
'lock' command calls VaultSession.lock() and prints: 'Vault locked.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 'privault audit' command and 'privault export' command
userStories.description: As a user, I need to review my access log and create encrypted backups.
acceptanceCriteria: 'audit' command reads AuditLog and prints all entries in a table (timestamp, action, entry_id, category)
'audit' prints warning if AuditTampered is raised
'export --out <path>' exports vault.db as an encrypted binary backup (just copies the file)
Export prints: 'Encrypted backup saved to <path>. Import with privault import.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add zero-network CI test and coverage gates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CI to verify: no outbound network calls, 90%+ coverage, mypy strict passes.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_network_isolation.py: patches socket.socket and verifies no test makes a network call
pytest.ini or pyproject.toml: addopts includes --cov=src/privault --cov-fail-under=90
mypy section in pyproject.toml: strict = true
.github/workflows/ci.yml: runs on push/PR, matrix Python 3.10/3.11/3.12
CI runs: black --check, isort --check-only, flake8, mypy, pytest
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Write README.md with threat model, install guide, and usage examples
userStories.description: As an open source user, I need a clear README so I understand what this protects against and how to use it.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md has sections: The Problem, What privault Does, Threat Model (table), Quickstart, Commands, How It Works (encryption chain), Non-Goals, Contributing
Quickstart section shows: pip install privault, privault init, privault add password, privault get github.com
Threat model table lists each threat and mitigation
Encryption section shows the Argon2id → AES-256-GCM chain in plain language
CI badge included
LICENSE file exists (MIT)
Typecheck passes
